title: Stages of the rasterization pipeline
description: Vertices flow through vertex shading, primitive assembly and clipping, rasterization to fragments, fragment shading, and per-fragment depth/blend tests.
body: Use when reasoning about where work happens in a frame. The fixed pipeline runs: vertex shader (per-vertex transform/attributes) -> primitive assembly and clipping -> rasterization (primitives to candidate fragments with interpolated attributes) -> fragment shader (per-fragment color) -> depth/stencil and blend tests -> framebuffer write. Vertex and fragment stages are programmable; the rest is fixed-function state. Do not expect to control rasterization order or fragment scheduling. Actions: place each computation in the cheapest correct stage (per-vertex when interpolation suffices). Validation signals include the GPU debugger showing expected inputs/outputs per stage. Risk boundary: doing per-vertex work per-fragment wastes huge throughput; locate work in the right stage and confirm via a capture.
